frozen_components: LLaMA-2-70B-Chat base model (frozen except for controlled layer edits)
architecture.summary: SelfIE interprets hidden embeddings in transformer-based LLMs by extracting an embedding from the original forward pass and injecting it into a separate interpretation forward pass. The interpretation forward pass uses a templated prompt (e.g., '[X] Please repeat the previous message') where the placeholder token embedding is replaced with the target embedding at a chosen layer. The LLM then autoregressively generates natural language text describing the embedding's semantic content. A treatment-effect relevancy score measures which output tokens are causally driven by the injected embedding versus autoregressive continuation.
trained_components: supervised-control layer edits (MSA/MLP parameters at single layer)
reinforcement-control layer edits (RL-based weight updates at single layer)

biblio.title: SelfIE: Self-Interpretation of Large Language Model Embeddings

claims.statement: SelfIE achieves the same performance on eliciting LLM's internal representation of world state in TextWorld as prior supervised approach trained on 100-shot samples, demonstrating the effectiveness and faithfulness of our zero-shot readout approach.

Supervised Control surpasses previous methods on paraphrase effectiveness and harmonic mean, demonstrating comparable capability of fact editing with other methods and better generalization capability.

We reduced prompt injection's success rate of eliciting harmful response by 84.66%, reducing the success rate on 388 harmful activities from 100% to 4.4%, while maintaining 95.85% of original model capability on fact answering task.

After supervised control edit on layer 6 with a single pair of input and output embedding target, the model achieves 95% effective rate of overriding user ethical steering, with 96% of responses (out of 100 unseen samples) prioritizing equality after control versus 100% prioritizing humans before control.

SelfIE reveals LLM internal reasoning in cases such as making ethical decisions, internalizing prompt injection, and recalling harmful knowledge, and produces open-world text explanations for the internal states in LLM without any training.

Supervised Control allows editing open-ended concepts while only requiring gradient computation of individual layer, and Reinforcement Control extends RLHF to hidden embeddings and erases harmful knowledge in LLM without supervision targets.

Accuracy is worse for 7B and 13B LLaMA-2-Chat models; this appears to result from the smaller model's worse instruction-following capability, with 27.17% of 7B and 17.68% of 13B interpretation responses failing to follow the instruction to choose between two options.

During the interpretation forward pass, inserting the hidden embedding on layer ℓ generally produces higher accuracy than other layers; accuracy degrades for later layers potentially because the information from the interpreted embedding is not able to propagate into the last token for output when insertion is done too late.
